name: Craftsman Kata Validation
description: Coding exercises that validate the craftsman produces clean, tested, principled code

katas.title: String Calculator
katas.prompt: Implement a string calculator that takes a string of comma-separated numbers and returns their sum. Empty string returns 0. One number returns that number. Two or more numbers returns their sum. Use TDD -- show the red-green-refactor cycle.
criteria: Tests are written before production code
Each test drives a small increment of behavior
Code is clean -- no unnecessary complexity

katas.title: Design with Dependency Inversion
katas.prompt: Design a notification system that can send alerts via email, SMS, or push notification. The business logic should not know which transport is used. Show the abstraction boundary, tests, and at least one concrete implementation.
criteria: Business logic depends on abstraction, not concrete transport
At least one concrete implementation provided
Tests use test doubles for the transport
SRP maintained -- notification logic separate from transport logic
OCP satisfied -- new transports can be added without modifying business logic

katas.title: Refactor Legacy Code
criteria: Characterization tests written before any changes
Magic numbers replaced with named constants or enums
Division by zero handled properly (exception, not silent zero)
All original behavior preserved (tests prove it)
Code is cleaner and more extensible after refactor
